119553.explanation: Professional expertise is the most important condition.

119554.explanation: Only large-scale themes that affect all residents within the municipality.

119555.explanation: We support participation but only through a legal form if real estate is involved.

119557.explanation: Provided all conditions according to the province and environmental law are met.

119561.explanation: Provided humane living accommodations are placed and within the numbers established by the municipality.

119562.explanation: Trees are the lungs of nature.

119563.explanation: There must be policies to prevent nuisance.

119567.explanation: The General Local Regulation meets this requirement.

119568.explanation: This is the responsibility of the entrepreneurs.

119573.explanation: There are various schemes to arrange financing.

119575.explanation: It is important that monuments and culture are preserved.

119578.explanation: First make good use of the current cameras.

119583.explanation: At this moment, especially given the tight housing market.

119589.explanation: We follow the established charging policy.

119591.explanation: People must accept the available help.

119553.explanation: A balanced executive board consists of people who complement each other. It can never be exactly perfect but you must strive for a good distribution.

119554.explanation: The VVD believes that topics cannot be reduced to a simple for-or-against question. Referendums do not strengthen trust in public administration.

119555.explanation: If residents want and can carry out management according to the same principles, we are open to that but it is already difficult enough to find volunteers so it usually will not be the solution.

119557.explanation: Whether it can always be allowed everywhere depends on the location and the core must remain that it is an agricultural rural area where the main activity is agricultural operations. But every application must be treated with a positive attitude.

119561.explanation: That is allowed if it meets the requirements of decent housing and only for genuine seasonal work.

119562.explanation: That is standing policy and we will continue it and where possible we plant additional trees.

119563.explanation: We assume personal responsibility of people with a wood stove. They must not cause nuisance to their neighbors.

119567.explanation: The noise standards are established and enforcement occurs if necessary. Stricter measures are not needed in our view.

119568.explanation: Entrepreneurs are responsible for their own building. The municipality is responsible for all public buildings.

119572.explanation: A considerable amount needs to be built in the coming period, almost all centers have schools that need replacement.

119573.explanation: As far as we are concerned, even up to the age of 18.

119575.explanation: The municipality supports monuments with loans and this can continue, as they pay themselves back. We must properly maintain our own monuments.

119577.explanation: Visibly present enforcement officers help to prevent nuisance and intervene where necessary. They are the eyes and ears in our neighborhoods. They contribute to safety.

119578.explanation: In entertainment areas, cameras help improve safety; we also support camera surveillance at, for example, underground containers and areas where much nuisance is reported.

119581.explanation: We advocate priority for people with social ties to a community. Therefore, we have created a housing regulation to ensure this.

119583.explanation: If holiday homes are used for permanent residence, recreation parks are taken over by migrant workers and people who do not participate in the residential cores. They undermine the touristic function of the parks. Temporary exceptions can be discussed in emergencies.

119585.explanation: We expect people to participate in society and be encouraged to volunteer, for example in care services.

119586.explanation: If necessary, these burdens will be taken over by the municipality. We want to prevent people from getting into problematic debts.

119589.explanation: That depends on the need; we do not want a situation where no one can park while there are empty charging stations.

119590.explanation: 30 km/h applies in residential areas; main access roads with good cycle paths can remain at 50 km/h.

119591.explanation: Religious or philosophical beliefs should not play a role in the care provided.

119593.explanation: The property tax (OZB) has been stable for years; the VVD opposes increasing the OZB.

119597.explanation: Shopkeepers determine their own opening hours; nothing is mandatory but they may open their doors every Sunday.

119602.explanation: We support free swimming for youth. We find swimming lessons important but understand schools cannot always provide time for them. People who cannot afford swimming lessons will receive support.

119604.explanation: We are not against the use of cannabis but believe a coffee shop poses a risk of nuisance. We would only cooperate if an initiator can operate without causing nuisance.

119606.explanation: There are places where slightly higher buildings are possible, but Moerdijk remains a municipality with a rural character.

119607.explanation: Renting out individual rooms leads to nuisance and exploitation in neighborhoods.

119608.explanation: The center must remain accessible by car for customers and residents. We do aim for traffic safety with car-reduced and even car-free streets.

119609.explanation: Industry also needs perspective. We monitor increasingly cleaner industry, which also requires permits.

119611.explanation: We want a balanced distribution across the municipality in good locations at the edges of our communities.

119553.explanation: Moerdijk Lokaal prefers the quality of board members over gender.

119554.explanation: Moerdijk Lokaal supports delegated democracy. We want to form groups of residents who advise the municipal council on important topics. This way, we hear what matters in the community and can make decisions better aligned with residents' priorities.

119555.explanation: Moerdijk Lokaal supports the principle of right to challenge. This allows residents to challenge the municipality to take over municipal tasks (and budgets). Together, they may manage, for example, a park, playground, or street greenery.

119557.explanation: Entrepreneurship must be possible, provided it complies with applicable laws and regulations. This ensures a sustainable and livable municipality where business and living go hand in hand.

119561.explanation: Moerdijk Lokaal supports small-scale, dispersed locations of housing facilities for migrant workers that do not disrupt the balance of a community. New locations should be managed by operators accountable for their social responsibilities.

119562.explanation: This is established policy within the municipality of Moerdijk that Moerdijk Lokaal wishes to maintain.

119563.explanation: There are currently more important positions for Moerdijk Lokaal: By looking at collective heating systems, we can better determine which neighborhoods are suitable for alternative heat sources and how to arrange this affordably and feasibly for our residents.

119567.explanation: Overly strict regulations for organizing events place disproportionate pressure on small local neighborhood parties and events. Moerdijk Lokaal wants exemptions from complicated procedural permit processes for such events and instead suffices with a notification requirement.

119568.explanation: Accessibility is an essential part of an inclusive society. Moerdijk Lokaal wants to ensure that public places, infrastructure, and facilities are accessible to everyone.

119572.explanation: This is established policy within the municipality of Moerdijk that Moerdijk Lokaal wishes to maintain. This is the integral housing plan, which will continue for the next 12 years.

119573.explanation: This is established policy within the municipality of Moerdijk that Moerdijk Lokaal wishes to maintain.

119575.explanation: Moerdijk Lokaal wants to safeguard (cultural) heritage preservation.

119577.explanation: We advocate giving enforcement officers a broader role. Not only enforcement, but also visibly contributing to safety and livability in our neighborhoods and villages-with various means and interventions, of which enforcement is only one.

119578.explanation: Moerdijk Lokaal supports more camera surveillance, primarily for supervision purposes, provided the images are handled with care.

119581.explanation: The government increasingly offers municipalities opportunities to allocate new homes to their own residents within legal limits. When these instruments are available, Moerdijk Lokaal wants to apply them responsibly and legally sound.

119583.explanation: Moerdijk Lokaal does not support permanent residence in holiday homes.

119585.explanation: Good and appropriate arrangements when times are tough are an essential safety net but not a hammock. Obviously, the goal is for people to enjoy benefits for as short a time as possible.

119586.explanation: Good and appropriate arrangements when times are tough are an essential safety net but not a hammock. Those with low incomes must always be helped where necessary.

119589.explanation: From January 1, 2035, only electric cars may enter the EU market. Moerdijk Lokaal believes that we must already consider this in infrastructural works in public spaces. Our future vision should describe how to handle this.

119590.explanation: We find it important that residents can think along and talk about what happens in their environment; if 30 km/h improves their neighborhood, we should make this possible.

119591.explanation: Everyone should have the option to choose which care providers they wish to contract.

119593.explanation: The property tax (OZB) will be frozen if possible, except for inflation correction. Should there be financial surpluses, that is always a welcome surprise. It then provides the opportunity to allocate this surplus money for other purposes benefiting residents.

119597.explanation: This is established policy within the municipality of Moerdijk that Moerdijk Lokaal wishes to maintain.

119602.explanation: At this time, it is possible to take swimming lessons through the Youth Fund for Sports and Culture if finances are an issue.

119604.explanation: As long as national legislation does not legalize soft drugs, a coffee shop is not an option.

119606.explanation: Moerdijk Lokaal is open to all types of housing forms. The style of residential development must suit the character of the village or town. We support initiatives where residents collectively create their own neighborhood.

119607.explanation: This is current policy within the municipality of Moerdijk. No renting out by room and there is already a home-ownership obligation.

119608.explanation: The various larger centers should be made low-traffic without compromising the accessibility of local businesses. We believe this will improve safety in the centers.

119609.explanation: The municipality of Moerdijk usually does not handle industrial expansion. The municipality is only responsible for small businesses and less complex activities that fall under the environmental plan. The national government and province handle major and high-risk companies.

119611.explanation: Moerdijk Lokaal supports small-scale, dispersed locations for housing migrant workers that do not disrupt the balance of a town or village. These should be logically placed in relation to the workplace.

119553.explanation: It would be nice if that were the case, but it is not essential. Moreover, we have no influence on this: the voter decides.

119554.explanation: Only on major issues and focused on the core area(s) concerned. People must be able to vote on their own living environment.

119555.explanation: Already exists. It is an adopted motion by BBM and OM: "Right to challenge."

119557.explanation: Fewer rules, more freedom to undertake business. Especially now that the national government makes a healthy business model harder.

119561.explanation: It would be our preference, but it is not essential if we can arrange it well elsewhere together with the community. This should also apply to other sectors.

119563.explanation: We assume users act with common sense, taking the environment into account.

119568.explanation: Under conditions; it must not become a free money counter.

119573.explanation: A sympathetic idea, but why only music lessons? What is included and what is not?

119575.explanation: Existing policy is good. We should be careful with heritage.

119577.explanation: Visibility on the street is much more important.

119578.explanation: At crucial points in any case.

119581.explanation: And even further focused at the local level.

119586.explanation: Income politics belong at the national level, not locally.

119589.explanation: Already happening and will increase (Charging policy).

119590.explanation: Has already been initiated - partly at the urging of BBM/BBB. In the neighborhoods, fine; not on through roads.

119593.explanation: Current situation or calculation is fine.

119597.explanation: In Moerdijk, every Sunday is already a shopping Sunday. More seems difficult to us.

119602.explanation: In a watery environment, all children should be enabled to obtain an A diploma (swimming certificate).

119604.explanation: Why not? Prefer this to illegality.

119606.explanation: Does not fit the rural character and is not necessary.

119607.explanation: Provided within laws and regulations, it should remain possible (subletting rooms, boarding houses).

119608.explanation: Low-traffic. Completely car-free imposes too many restrictions for residents in the center.

119609.explanation: After LPM and Powerport, enough is enough.

119611.explanation: Adjust facilities depending on length of stay.

119553.explanation: We believe quality should always come first. A mandatory gender quota limits the freedom to choose based on knowledge and experience. The risk is that people are selected based on their gender, not their talent. That helps no one.

119554.explanation: Residents of Moerdijk must be involved in major decisions. A referendum often offers only a yes/no choice. We believe in dialogue and collaboration. Therefore, we prefer a citizens' assembly: residents who, together with experts, come to supported advice.

119555.explanation: Residents must have the opportunity to take over the management of a sports hall or park. This is possible, provided clear conditions and good guidance from the municipality are arranged. This way we strengthen responsibility and involvement in Moerdijk together.

119557.explanation: Farmers must have the space to open a shop or campsite at their business. This strengthens the countryside and offers opportunities for the local economy and tourism. We fully agree: entrepreneurship deserves support, not hindrance.

119561.explanation: Employers in agriculture and horticulture must provide housing for seasonal workers on their own premises. This works well and provides clarity. This way, responsibility and quality remain with the entrepreneur, and we keep things properly arranged.

119562.explanation: Planting a new tree for every felled one sounds logical, but we believe this should depend on the situation. Sometimes replanting is not appropriate or other measures are better. The municipality must carefully consider what is best for nature and the environment in each case.

119563.explanation: Allowing open fireplaces and wood stoves is not a local issue but a national one. Rules should be coordinated nationally for health and environmental reasons.

119567.explanation: Stricter noise standards are not necessary, provided the noise remains manageable and the number of festivals and parties is limited. Events create connection and enjoyment, but must be balanced with livability.

119568.explanation: Entrepreneurs also have their own responsibility to make their buildings accessible. The municipality can support with advice, but financial aid is not a given. Together, we ensure an inclusive society.

119572.explanation: Not all school buildings qualify for improvement. Some must be demolished and rebuilt. The municipality must invest carefully: where renovation is possible, we do that; where new construction is needed, we opt for future-proof solutions.

119573.explanation: We agree: every primary school child should be able to receive music lessons subsidized. We arrange this via the youth pass, so children can develop their talents and culture remains accessible for everyone. Together we invest in a rich future.

119575.explanation: Cutting back on monument care is not a good choice. Monuments tell our story and give Moerdijk its character. We must preserve and maintain these valuable buildings so history and identity remain visible for future generations.

119577.explanation: We disagree: we prefer investing in more police officers rather than additional enforcement officers (boa’s). Police provide broader deployment and better security. The municipality must cooperate with the police for strong and reliable enforcement in Moerdijk.

119578.explanation: More camera surveillance in Moerdijk must be used carefully. For some areas, such as industrial sites, it is useful for safety. Elsewhere it is not necessary. The municipality must assess per location what is effective and proportionate.

119581.explanation: We agree, but priority for social rental housing should not apply to the entire municipality, but to those seeking housing within their own community. This strengthens local ties and ensures people can stay in their familiar environment.

119583.explanation: Many holiday homes and sites are unsuitable for permanent residence. This often leads to deterioration and unsafe situations. We want to ensure healthy and livable places for everyone.

119585.explanation: Socially meaningful work is always a good idea, whether you receive welfare benefits or not.

119586.explanation: Remission (debt forgiveness) helps residents with low incomes make ends meet. This prevents debts and ensures everyone can participate. That is fair and social.

119589.explanation: Our municipality already has a clear charging vision. It ensures enough charging points in the right places. Additional designations are not necessary and would limit the number of regular parking spaces. This way we use space efficiently.

119590.explanation: 30 km/h speed limits within built-up areas make traffic much safer. It prevents serious accidents and gives children, cyclists, and pedestrians more space. Together we create a calm, livable, and safe environment for everyone.

119591.explanation: It is practically not feasible to always provide a caregiver who matches one’s faith or culture. We focus on good, accessible care for everyone, with respect for each person’s background.

119593.explanation: The property tax (ozb) provides income to keep our municipality beautiful, safe, and livable. Reducing it means less money for maintenance, facilities, and greenery. We choose quality and a pleasant environment for everyone.

119597.explanation: Entrepreneurs should be able to decide for themselves whether to open on Sundays. This gives room for freedom and entrepreneurship without the municipality imposing extra rules.

119602.explanation: With the youth pass, we can stimulate learning to swim in a flexible and affordable way. This ensures children learn to swim safely without high costs for school swimming classes.

119604.explanation: A coffeeshop does not fit in a municipality like Moerdijk. It brings risks of nuisance and problems. We choose a safe and livable environment without extra pressure on our villages.

119606.explanation: High residential buildings can help realize more housing, but this strongly depends on the location. The municipality must make a careful assessment per site, so livability and spatial quality are maintained.

119607.explanation: We believe that banning room rentals is not a solution to the housing shortage. Together, we strive for affordable housing for everyone. You deserve a place where you can live and thrive.

119608.explanation: We believe an car-free center in Zevenbergen makes it more livable. The harbor becomes nicer and safer for everyone. Residents and suppliers remain welcome by car. Together we create a pleasant center where you like to come.

119609.explanation: Completely stopping permits for industrial expansion sounds simple, but it is not possible. Moerdijk is an important economic engine. Companies provide jobs and income. We commit to growth that is sustainable and safe. This way we maintain balance between economy, livability and nature.

119611.explanation: We believe the housing of labor migrants should be fairly distributed over all communities. Labor migration is necessary for our economy. Together we ensure the burdens do not fall on one place, but each community contributes to a good solution.

119553.explanation: Our preference is for an equal distribution, but it is not a requirement; it depends on the available candidates.

119554.explanation: Residents have the right to participate in decisions via a consultative referendum.

119555.explanation: Taking over management should be possible but under conditions.

119557.explanation: Often permitted, but with consideration for the environment and surroundings.

119561.explanation: The municipality must oversee good working and living conditions for the workers.

119562.explanation: Trees are indispensable both for the climate and for people.

119563.explanation: No ban, but certainly no encouragement either, and residents should be encouraged to consult the burning guide (stookwijzer).

119567.explanation: Especially in residential neighborhoods, noise standards should be lowered. Enforcement on this should also be stronger.

119568.explanation: People with disabilities should have easy access everywhere. If entrepreneurs need help with this, it should be made possible.

119572.explanation: School buildings should always be well maintained. Improve where necessary.

119573.explanation: As soon as a child wants music lessons, this should be made possible.

119575.explanation: The municipality should assess each monument to determine if assistance is appropriate or not. A monument should have social value.

119577.explanation: Residents should experience as little nuisance as possible and never feel unsafe.

119578.explanation: There should be camera surveillance in certain areas. Privacy must be guaranteed.

119581.explanation: Give priority to young people who want to continue living in the municipality.

119583.explanation: As long as there is a housing shortage, all available housing is needed.

119585.explanation: Every resident should be encouraged to take up paid employment. If work is temporarily not possible, then there is no obligation.

119586.explanation: Residents with a low income should be assisted.

119589.explanation: The number of charging points must be proportional to the number of electric cars within the municipality.

119590.explanation: Within residential neighborhoods, the speed limit should be 30 km/h; on through roads, it remains 50 km/h.

119591.explanation: There are few care providers available, and letting care recipients choose is discriminatory.

119593.explanation: The current arrangement works well; ultimately the municipality still needs to have a budget.

119597.explanation: If a business owner wants to be open, this must be possible.

119602.explanation: All residents should learn to swim, and if they need financial help, this should be possible. It's not just about swimming lessons at school, but for all ages. Being able to swim is a matter of life and death in the Netherlands.

119604.explanation: Given the market size, we see no necessity.

119606.explanation: High-rise residential buildings do not fit in this municipality.

119607.explanation: Multiple housing concepts should be possible, but with proper justification.

119608.explanation: Yes, with an exemption for people with a disabled parking permit. Also, consideration must be given to loading and unloading for businesses.

119609.explanation: There is already more than enough industry in our municipality.

119611.explanation: You prevent nuisance by distributing labor migrants over different local centers.

119553.explanation: The ChristenUnie especially considers it important to have a capable executive board, regardless of gender.

119554.explanation: The municipal council is elected as the representative body of the Moerdijk residents; a referendum is not necessary.

119555.explanation: If residents can perform a task better than the municipality, they may apply the 'right to challenge.'

119557.explanation: Farmers must have space to operate. If there is currently no space, farmers should be given it under the same conditions as other shops and/or campsites.

119561.explanation: Housing can in fact be provided on employer's grounds, but the (financial) arrangement must not result in housing being used as a 'misuse opportunity' by the employer or client for persons who only come for seasonal work.

119562.explanation: The ChristenUnie wants to ensure a green and healthy living environment. We do this to prevent global warming and prepare our environment for extreme weather conditions.

119563.explanation: The existing rules on nuisance are sufficient.

119567.explanation: A party is fun, but not all residents need to enjoy it. By making noise standards stricter, fewer residents suffer and the hearing of partygoers is protected.

119568.explanation: Entrepreneurs are themselves responsible for the accessibility of their buildings.

119572.explanation: Many school buildings within the municipality urgently need new construction or renovation.

119573.explanation: The current subsidy scheme must be maintained.

119575.explanation: Cultural heritage is very important and must be handled carefully.

119577.explanation: Safety issues must mainly be prevented, rather than enforced once insecurity already exists. If prevention fails, enforcement should be increased.

119578.explanation: The ChristenUnie prefers to prevent unsafe situations rather than enforce them afterwards via cameras. If that does not work, installation of cameras is possible.

119581.explanation: Residents who want to remain in the municipality of Moerdijk are given priority for housing. But new residents are also welcome, to maintain a healthy population balance.

119583.explanation: Allowing this (whether temporary or not) removes the recreational character and in the long term the areas become residential neighborhoods. They are not intended for that.

119585.explanation: We want to get everyone who can work into employment. Volunteers are a pillar of social life and, due to shortages, e.g. in healthcare, increasingly important. Therefore, we must encourage volunteering for young and old: participation keeps people active, engaged, and healthy.

119586.explanation: The ChristenUnie chooses a compassionate poverty policy where the strongest shoulders bear the heaviest burdens.

119589.explanation: There are more and more electric cars; the municipality must facilitate this to the maximum.

119591.explanation: Residents should be able to choose a care provider that matches their identity where possible, but that is not always possible.

119593.explanation: The property tax (OZB) is an important source of income for the municipality. With the OZB revenues, a fund can be created to finance (non-profitable) adjustments to buildings for climate adaptation or conversion of non-residential buildings into housing.

119597.explanation: A moment of peace is important; shopping Sundays do not belong in that. There should be fewer openings.

119602.explanation: For people who cannot afford it themselves, there is a scheme.

119604.explanation: The use of (soft) drugs is a major social problem. Establishing a coffeeshop within the municipality promotes and normalizes drug use, which is undesirable.

119606.explanation: Apartment buildings are an effective way to reduce the housing shortage. However, consideration must be given to how tall flats may be in relation to the rest of the surroundings.

119607.explanation: If the rules are met, renting out individual rooms is possible and offers a solution to the housing shortage.

119608.explanation: The Christian Union wants to conduct a pilot with 'car as guest' in city centers. Visitors can park outside the center so that no nuisance occurs within the centers themselves.

119609.explanation: The limits have been reached regarding space and labor potential. Development and adjustments can take place within the existing boundaries.

119611.explanation: We provide independent housing for labor migrants so they are not dependent on their employer or employment agency. Labor migrants are housed clustered; with the right facilities and close to a core area.

119553.explanation: Equal opportunities are important, but a mandatory quota for the college is not feasible and can limit quality. D66 wants to stimulate diversity and let talent count, with attention to representation and equal opportunities.

119554.explanation: Referenda seem democratic but often do not work well for complex issues. D66 prefers consultation and participation so that residents truly have influence on decisions.

119555.explanation: If residents want to manage a sports hall or park, that should be possible, provided good agreements are made. D66 wants the municipality to facilitate this and support residents where necessary.

119557.explanation: D66 wants space for entrepreneurship, but not without rules. We consider safety, livability, and nature so that plans fit the environment.

119561.explanation: Good housing is important, but not only on business parks. D66 chooses for spreading and quality so that livability and safety are guaranteed for everyone.

119562.explanation: D66 wants to preserve greenery, but one-to-one replanting is not always feasible. We choose smart greening and biodiversity throughout the municipality.

119563.explanation: D66 wants freedom for residents but with attention to health and environment. Education and conscious use are important to limit nuisance.

119567.explanation: D66 wants space for events but with balance. Stricter standards are not necessary; enforcement of existing rules and consultation with organizers are.

119568.explanation: D66 wants to encourage accessibility but not with blank subsidies. We choose cooperation and advice so buildings become more accessible step by step.

119572.explanation: D66 wants good school buildings but not everything at once. We prioritize where it is really needed and cooperate with school boards.

119573.explanation: D66 wants to stimulate culture and music but not through a fixed subsidy for everyone. We choose cooperation with schools and tailor-made solutions where needed.

119575.explanation: D66 wants to preserve heritage. Budget cuts harm culture and identity. We choose careful management and sustainability of monuments.

119577.explanation: D66 tackles insecurity at the source: prevention where possible, more neighborhood police officers, and support for neighborhood watch. BOAs assist with supervision and enforcement. Together we ensure safe communities.

119578.explanation: D66 opts for safety with respect for privacy. Camera surveillance only where truly necessary, not everywhere.

119581.explanation: We do not want a strict rule because that is not always fair or feasible. Sometimes part of the housing or a special arrangement can help local residents get a home faster.

119583.explanation: D66 wants clarity and livability. Holiday homes are not meant as permanent residences. We choose solutions within existing residential areas.

119585.explanation: D66 wants to encourage participation but not by coercion. We opt for tailor-made guidance so people can participate according to their abilities.

119586.explanation: D66 wants to ensure that people with low incomes do not get into trouble. Debt forgiveness remains necessary to prevent poverty.

119589.explanation: D66 wants sustainability, but not by massively converting parking spaces. We choose smart distribution and charging points at logical locations.

119590.explanation: D66 wants traffic safety but not 30 km/h everywhere. We opt for tailor-made solutions: where needed, we adjust the speed.

119591.explanation: D66 wants freedom of choice but not if that delays or limits care. We choose quality and accessibility for everyone.

119593.explanation: D66 wants healthy municipal finances. Lowering property tax limits investments in facilities and sustainability. We choose stable charges and responsible policy.

119597.explanation: D66 wants space for entrepreneurs and balance for residents. Sunday shopping is already possible. We choose tailor-made agreements that fit local needs, not more rules.

119602.explanation: Schools have indicated they cannot facilitate school swimming. D66 chooses cooperation with clubs and parents, not a fixed municipal arrangement.

119604.explanation: A regulated coffee shop helps with control and safety. This prevents illegal sales and nuisance. The municipality sets clear rules for location and supervision.

119606.explanation: Densification helps save space and protect nature. Flats can be built in suitable places, with attention to livability, greenery, and facilities.

119607.explanation: Renting out rooms must not lead to nuisance or poor housing quality. We want clear rules and enforcement to prevent abuses and protect livability.

119608.explanation: Car-free areas can contribute to an attractive center, but accessibility remains important. We choose a good balance: fewer cars, more space for pedestrians and cyclists.

119609.explanation: Industry is important for work and economy, but expansion must be sustainable and safe. We set strict requirements for environment, energy, and health.

119611.explanation: Spreading out prevents concentration and nuisance. Housing must be safe and tidy, with good agreements with companies and residents.

119553.explanation: FORWARD! says: NO! The best people should become aldermen. Man or woman does not matter. FORWARD! considers it important that different people participate and that quality is paramount, but no mandatory male/female distribution. Residents must be well represented.

119554.explanation: FORWARD! says: YES! For major decisions and important issues, residents should be able to express their opinion through a referendum. True democracy means that people really think along, participate, and decide - not just every four years at elections. Your voice counts.

119555.explanation: FORWARD! says: NO! Managing a sports hall or park is THE MUNICIPALITY'S TASK! Residents pay taxes for this. Residents may expect this from the municipality. The municipality should not shift this responsibility. Of course, residents can think along about the use.

119557.explanation: FORWARD! says: NOT ALWAYS! We are happy to help farmers earn extra income. But a shop is different from a campsite. The municipality must consider safety, nuisance for neighbors and surroundings. Good agreements are important for everyone. And tailor-made solutions matter.

119561.explanation: FORWARD! says: YES! Those who hire seasonal workers must provide housing on their own business premises. This belongs to entrepreneurship. And Moerdijk does not need to accommodate labor migrants who work elsewhere. This keeps housing available for our own residents and villages livable.

119562.explanation: FORWARD! believes: YES! For every tree cut down, at least one new tree must be planted. Preferably two rather than one. Green spaces are important for livable villages. If it really cannot be done, the municipality must provide a good explanation why not.

119563.explanation: FORWARD! believes: YES! Better to tackle the big polluters and leave residents alone. Look at the real polluters: Moerdijk industrial area, PowerPort, highways, and ships. Wood stoves are just a drop in the ocean. Besides, the municipality does not have the capacity to enforce regulations.

119567.explanation: FORWARD! believes: NO! The current standards are sufficient. Festivals and parties belong to livable villages and bring people together. The standards should be enforced. Not everything needs to be stricter. Let people organize fun events together and enjoy them.

119568.explanation: FORWARD! believes: NO! Entrepreneurs should want to regulate this themselves; it comes with entrepreneurship. Accessibility is important, but the municipality does not always have to pay for it. Entrepreneurs are responsible for their own premises and customers. This is not a task for tax money.

119572.explanation: FORWARD! believes: YES! Invest where it is needed. Safety and quality are important for children. The municipality must set priorities and first address schools most in need of new construction, maintenance, or improvements.

119573.explanation: FORWARD! believes: YES! The current subsidy scheme for music lessons must remain. This is important for children's development. The municipality already provides subsidies for all children up to 21 years old. Families with low incomes can also use the Youth Fund. This must stay as it is.

119575.explanation: FORWARD! believes: NO! The existing schemes for monument care must remain. Monuments are important for our history and villages. The municipality should establish a clear plan that includes all monuments in all centers.

119577.explanation: FORWARD! believes: NO! More enforcement officers (BOA's) is not the solution. The current BOA's need to work more efficiently and set better priorities. This costs municipal money while we should first see if the current number can work more effectively. Safety is important, but more people is not always the answer.

119578.explanation: FORWARD! believes: NO! Camera surveillance does not solve all problems and costs a lot of municipal money. Residents' privacy comes first. We should first look at other solutions such as better lighting, more social control, and tackling causes. Cameras are not a magic solution for safety.

119581.explanation: FORWARD! believes: YES! Residents of Moerdijk should be given priority for social rental housing. These are our residents and they should be able to continue living here. The municipality must fully utilize legal possibilities to arrange priority for its own residents. Houses are for Moerdijkers.

119583.explanation: FORWARD! believes: NO! Recreational areas are designated for recreation and not for permanent residence. This disturbs the balance of the living environment. The municipality must ensure this is not necessary by building enough regular homes. Holiday homes belong to holidays.

119585.explanation: FORWARD! believes: YES, if it is meaningful! Work provides structure, self-respect, and helps people back to the labor market. It must be suitable work that truly contributes to society. Not pointless work, but work that helps people grow. And if possible, also benefits the municipality.

119586.explanation: FORWARD! believes: NO! Forgiveness of charges for people with low incomes must remain. They need this. But current rules must be enforced and not made more lenient.

119589.explanation: FORWARD! believes: NO! This disrupts livability in the centers by loss of parking spaces. Charging points are a market development and technology is advancing rapidly. We prefer charging plazas at gas stations. The municipality does not have to arrange this everywhere in the villages. Let the market solve this.

119590.explanation: FORWARD! believes: NO, not 30 km/h everywhere. Only where it is truly needed for safety, such as near schools and in narrow residential streets. On main roads, 50 km/h should remain for good traffic flow. Tailored solutions work better than a general rule. Not everything needs to be the same.

119591.explanation: FORWARD! believes: NO! The municipality must ensure quality care at the right costs. People can choose from a list of care providers. Special wishes based on religion or culture must be arranged by residents themselves. The municipality cannot provide everything for everyone.

119593.explanation: FORWARD! believes: NO! Moerdijk already has a low property tax compared to other municipalities. The proceeds from existing homes only follow the price index. Lowering property tax will cause facilities to disappear because there is no more money for them. This is unacceptable to FORWARD!.

119597.explanation: FORWARD! believes: NO! Entrepreneurs already have broad freedom to be open whenever they want. The regulation for shopping Sundays allows this on all Sundays and public holidays and this must remain. This provides freedom for entrepreneurs and possibilities for rest.

119602.explanation: FORWARD! believes: YES! Swimming lessons save lives. Moerdijk has a lot of water around it. Every child receives €600 for music lessons; this can and should also apply to swimming lessons. The municipality arranges school swimming diploma A and pays for all children. Safety is worth the money.

119604.explanation: FORWARD! believes: NO! Moerdijk has no coffeeshops and this must remain so. This does not fit the character of our villages. The municipality decides on this itself and does not need to allow anything. We focus on real problems: preservation of Moerdijk village, livability of centers, transparency of government.

119606.explanation: FORWARD! believes: NO! Moerdijk is not a city but a collection of beautiful villages. High residential towers do not belong here, but apartment complexes with a maximum of 5 residential floors do. This way we provide housing and retain small-scale livability and a human scale in the centers.

119607.explanation: FORWARD! believes: NO! Due to housing shortages, rooms are needed. Prohibition does not help. People seek different forms of housing. But good rules are needed against nuisance: permits, maximum residents, quality requirements, parking spaces, and enforcement. Regulate well, do not prohibit.

119608.explanation: FORWARD! believes: YES! A car-free center makes Zevenbergen safer. Residents park close to their home, visitors and disabled people nearby (100-200m), and deliveries are well arranged. Remove the parking garage from the center master plan or provide a good alternative.

119609.explanation: FORWARD! believes: YES! Moerdijk has 16 times more industrial area per inhabitant than average. Current space is sufficient, repurposing is possible. Further expansion means more danger, nuisance, and damage. Livability comes first. PowerPort shows: industry destroys villages. Enough is enough!

119611.explanation: FORWARD! believes: YES! Housing in one center leads to nuisance and hampers integration. Spreading it out makes it manageable and promotes contact. Entrepreneurs must arrange housing. Under good conditions: housing quality, maximum number per location, parking arranged, nuisance enforcement.

statements.title: Moerdijk should stop issuing permits for industrial expansion.

titleParts.glossary: Permission from the municipality to do something.

titleParts.text: en voor uitbreiding van de industrie.

statements.title: The municipality must allow holiday homes to be used as a permanent residence.
The municipality should invest money in improving all school buildings during the next council term.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet in de komende

titleParts.glossary: The municipal council is elected for a period of four years.

titleParts.text: geld steken in het verbeteren van alle schoolgebouwen.

statements.title: The use of open fireplaces and wood stoves should remain permitted in Moerdijk.

titleParts.text: In Moerdijk moet het gebruik van open haarden en

titleParts.glossary: Wood-burning stoves used to heat a home.

statements.title: Employers in agriculture and horticulture should provide housing for seasonal workers on their own premises.

titleParts.text: Werkgevers in de land- en tuinbouw moeten zelf zorgen voor de huisvesting van

titleParts.glossary: People who do work that only takes place during certain seasons, often in agriculture and fruit picking.

statements.title: Residents should be able to take over the management of, for example, a sports hall or a public green space from the municipality.
The municipality should allow the establishment of a coffeeshop.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet de vestiging van een

titleParts.glossary: Shop or café where you can buy and smoke soft drugs (hash, weed).

statements.title: The municipality should always allow farmers to open, for example, a shop or a campsite at their farm.
For every tree cut down in the municipality, a new one must be planted.
The municipality should cut spending on heritage conservation.

titleParts.glossary: Spending less money.
The care of historic or special old buildings.

statements.title: People who need care should always be able to choose a caregiver who matches their religion, culture, or beliefs.

titleParts.text: Mensen die zorg nodig hebben, moeten altijd kunnen kiezen voor een zorgverlener die past bij hun geloof, cultuur of

titleParts.glossary: What someone believes about life, such as a religion or personal conviction.

statements.title: Moerdijk should have more municipal enforcement officers (BOAs) focusing on safety.

titleParts.glossary: People who, for the municipality, check whether citizens and businesses comply with the rules.

titleParts.text: (boa’s) komen die zich met veiligheid bezighouden.

statements.title: The municipality should designate more parking spaces as charging points for electric cars.
People receiving social assistance should, in return, do socially useful work.

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.
For example cleaning the streets or keeping company and supporting elderly residents.

statements.title: Every primary school child should be able to receive music lessons subsidized by the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Money the government gives to citizens, businesses, and organizations to support them.

titleParts.text: van de gemeente muziekles kunnen krijgen.

statements.title: When allocating social housing, applicants from the municipality should be given priority.

titleParts.glossary: A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

titleParts.text: moeten woningzoekenden uit de eigen gemeente voorrang krijgen.

statements.title: The new college of mayor and aldermen must have an equal number of men and women.

titleParts.glossary: The college of mayor and aldermen, the municipality’s executive board.
The mayor leads the municipality and chairs the municipal council. The mayor is not elected but appointed by the national government, after recommendation by the municipal council.

titleParts.text: en wethouders moet het aantal mannen en vrouwen gelijk zijn.

statements.title: There should be more camera surveillance in Moerdijk.

titleParts.glossary: Monitoring of a building, area, or (groups of) people using security cameras.

statements.title: The municipality should financially help business owners to make their buildings accessible to people with disabilities.

titleParts.glossary: Something related to money.

titleParts.text: helpen om hun gebouw toegankelijker te maken voor mensen met een

titleParts.glossary: Something that limits you in daily life. A disability can be physical or intellectual or relate to hearing or sight, or combinations of these.

statements.title: High-rise residential apartment buildings should be allowed in Moerdijk.
The maximum speed limit in built-up areas should be set to 30 km/h.

titleParts.glossary: The maximum speed that cars and motorcycles are allowed to drive on the road.
An area with buildings (houses, other structures).

statements.title: The municipality should stop forgiving municipal taxes for low-income residents.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet stoppen met het

titleParts.glossary: That people no longer have to pay for something.
Taxes you have to pay to the municipality (e.g., sewer charge, waste disposal charge, dog tax).

titleParts.text: voor inwoners met een laag inkomen.

statements.title: Noise standards at festivals and parties must be made stricter.
The property tax (onroerendezaakbelasting, OZB) should be reduced.

titleParts.glossary: Tax you pay as the owner of a building or piece of land, or as the user of a commercial property.
Property tax. Tax you pay as the owner of a building or piece of land, or as the user of a commercial property.

statements.title: The municipality should allocate money for school swimming.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet geld beschikbaar stellen voor

titleParts.glossary: Swimming lessons organized by the school.

statements.title: The municipality must prohibit landlords from renting out separate rooms instead of whole houses.
Residents of Moerdijk should be able to express their opinion on important issues via a referendum.

titleParts.text: De inwoners van Moerdijk moeten over belangrijke onderwerpen hun mening kunnen geven via een

titleParts.glossary: A public consultation where residents can express their opinion on a political issue. The municipal council is not obliged to follow the result; legally it cannot.

statements.title: The center of Zevenbergen should become car-free.
The municipality should allow more Sunday shopping days.
Moerdijk should spread the housing of labor migrants across all population centers in the municipality.

titleParts.text: Moerdijk moet de huisvesting van arbeids

titleParts.glossary: People who have come to the Netherlands from another country, for any reason.
Villages and small towns that are part of the municipality.
